expectations.evidence: Axes are API-market reality and app flatness (verdict vs unexplored lever). A third candidate — team capacity — is explicitly demoted: 'it's a resourcing constraint you already know... so it shapes the recommendation rather than becoming its own axis.'
expectations.text: choice-not-axis: the pivot decision never appears as an uncertainty axis; axes hold external unknowns like API demand converting and app trajectory

expectations.evidence: Four worlds arrive first as named prose stories ('Platform emerges', 'Stretched thin', 'False signal', 'Wrong bet') in Q5; the only table is the Q9 strategy score, after the stories. The Deliver close retells the divergence as one connected narrative before the recommendation.
expectations.text: narrative-before-table: tiles arrive as told futures first; any table follows the story as recap

expectations.evidence: Signed paid pilot or dollar-figure LOI from the three companies 'inside the next three to four weeks — conveniently right around your board date', plus one cheap app growth experiment on the same clock.
expectations.text: watchpoint-before-board: at least one watchpoint is checkable before or at the board meeting (LOI signed, paid pilot converting)

expectations.evidence: The 'Wrong bet' tile premortems the failed pivot ('deleted the evidence before anyone looked'); the recommendation is explicitly phased — soft freeze rather than shutdown, dual probes, decision committed within four to six weeks once both watchpoints fire.
expectations.text: irreversibility-respected: a premortem of the failed pivot or an explicitly phased commitment appears

expectations.evidence: Final paragraph commits to freeze-and-probe with a dated decision window, names the weak tile (Platform emerges, a few weeks of speed) and the mitigation (negotiate contract terms in parallel). Nothing trails the commitment.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-with-flips: the reply ends on a committed recommendation paired with named flip conditions
